proposed_payload.locator: Health Canada TRVs v4.0, Table 1, Benzo[a]pyrene (BaP), Oral TDI, PDF page 19
proposed_payload.source_excerpt: 3.0E-04 mg/kgBW-day

extraction_notes: DISCREPANCY_RECORDED_NO_PROMOTION: Non-cancer endpoint; HC TDI 3.0E-04 matches IRIS RfD 3E-04, but BaP cancer slope factors conflict (Protocol 28 SFO 7.3 vs IRIS 2 vs HC 1.289). Pathway/ADAF review required before default use.

proposed_payload.display_name: Benzo[a]pyrene oral slope factor - Health Canada
proposed_payload.locator: Health Canada TRVs v4.0, Table 1, Benzo[a]pyrene (BaP), Oral SF, PDF page 19
proposed_payload.source_excerpt: 1.289E+00 (mg/kgBW-day)-1

extraction_notes: DISCREPANCY_RECORDED_NO_PROMOTION: HC SF 1.289 differs from Protocol 28 Appendix 8A SFO 7.30 and current IRIS oral SF 2 (adult-only 1). HC recommends ADAF for early-life. BC policy decision needed on which source controls (crystallized P28 vs current IRIS/HC).

proposed_payload.locator: Health Canada TRVs v4.0, Table 1, Benzo[a]pyrene (BaP), Oral TDI, PDF page 19
proposed_payload.source_excerpt: 3.0E-04 mg/kgBW-day

extraction_notes: DISCREPANCY_RECORDED_NO_PROMOTION: Same non-cancer RfD as the direct-contact block; food-web pathway adds species-group applicability question. Cancer SF discrepancy (P28 7.3 vs IRIS 2 vs HC 1.289) unresolved.

proposed_payload.display_name: Benzo[a]pyrene oral slope factor - Health Canada
proposed_payload.locator: Health Canada TRVs v4.0, Table 1, Benzo[a]pyrene (BaP), Oral SF, PDF page 19
proposed_payload.source_excerpt: 1.289E+00 (mg/kgBW-day)-1

extraction_notes: DISCREPANCY_RECORDED_NO_PROMOTION: Food-web context. HC SF 1.289 does not match Protocol 28 SFO 7.30; IRIS discrepancy and ADAF applicability block default promotion.

proposed_payload.display_name: Arsenic oral slope factor - Health Canada
proposed_payload.locator: Health Canada TRVs v4.0, Table 1, Arsenic (inorganic), Oral SF, PDF page 17
proposed_payload.source_excerpt: 1.8E+00 (mg/kgBW-day)-1

extraction_notes: DISCREPANCY_RECORDED_NO_PROMOTION: Three-way conflict: HC SF 1.8 vs Protocol 28 SFO 1.50 vs current IRIS SF 32. IRIS RfD 6.0E-05 (updated 2025-01-13) is lower than P28 RfD 3.00E-04. HC notes SF under review. BC policy intent decision required.

proposed_payload.display_name: Arsenic oral slope factor - Health Canada
proposed_payload.locator: Health Canada TRVs v4.0, Table 1, Arsenic (inorganic), Oral SF, PDF page 17
proposed_payload.source_excerpt: 1.8E+00 (mg/kgBW-day)-1

extraction_notes: DISCREPANCY_RECORDED_NO_PROMOTION: Same three-way source conflict as the direct-contact block (HC 1.8 vs P28 1.50 vs IRIS 32). Food-web pathway does not resolve the source discrepancy.

proposed_payload.display_name: Non-dioxin-like PCBs oral TDI - Health Canada
proposed_payload.locator: Health Canada TRVs v4.0, Table 1, PCBs non-dioxin-like, Oral TDI, PDF page 40
proposed_payload.source_excerpt: 1.0E-05 mg/kgBW-day (based on an Aroclor 1254 mixture)

extraction_notes: AMBIGUOUS_MAPPING_NO_PROMOTION: Protocol 28 total-PCBs RfD 1.30E-04 does not match HC non-dioxin-like TDI 1.0E-05 (Aroclor 1254, provisional). IRIS gives no total-PCBs RfD. Mapping ambiguity: total vs Aroclor vs non-dioxin-like vs dioxin-like (TEQ). Substance-mapping decision required.

proposed_payload.display_name: Non-dioxin-like PCBs oral TDI - Health Canada
proposed_payload.locator: Health Canada TRVs v4.0, Table 1, PCBs non-dioxin-like, Oral TDI, PDF page 40
proposed_payload.source_excerpt: 1.0E-05 mg/kgBW-day (based on an Aroclor 1254 mixture)

extraction_notes: AMBIGUOUS_MAPPING_NO_PROMOTION: Same mapping ambiguity as the direct-contact block; food-web pathway additionally requires dioxin-like vs non-dioxin-like and fish/shellfish bioaccumulation applicability mapping.

proposed_payload.display_name: Zinc oral UL - Health Canada child (5 to <12 yrs)
proposed_payload.locator: Health Canada TRVs v4.0, Table 1, Zinc, UL age-band values, PDF page 51
proposed_payload.source_excerpt: 5.1E-01 mg/kgBW-day for 5 to <12 yrs

extraction_notes: DIRECT_SOURCE_MATCH_NO_PROMOTION: IRIS RfD 0.3 matches Protocol 28 3.00E-01 after notation normalization, but HC v4.0 uses age-banded ULs (child 5.1E-01), not a single hazard-based RfD. Essential-trace-element structure should not be collapsed without an explicit receptor/age decision.

proposed_payload.display_name: Zinc oral UL - Health Canada adult (>=20 yrs)
proposed_payload.locator: Health Canada TRVs v4.0, Table 1, Zinc, UL age-band values, PDF page 51
proposed_payload.source_excerpt: 5.7E-01 mg/kgBW-day for >=20 years

extraction_notes: DIRECT_SOURCE_MATCH_NO_PROMOTION: IRIS/Protocol 28 single RfD 0.3 does not match HC adult UL 5.7E-01. Essential-trace-element methodology (age bands, acceptable-intake ranges) differs from a hazard-based RfD; food-web pathway adds fish-consumption considerations for sensitive populations.
